notes:
PASS. All CRITICAL and HIGH checks clean.

--- CRITICAL checks ---

DELETION (PASS): The v1 radial blob pattern — the 3-stack g.fillCircle(x,y,baseR) / g.fillCircle(x,y,baseR*0.72) / g.fillCircle(x,y,baseR*0.45) per-location loop — is fully removed from WorldMapScene.js. The 12-seed deterministic desert stipple loop (const seeds = [[-0.6,-0.3],[0.4,-0.5],...]) is also gone. The two fillCircle calls remaining in the file (WorldMapScene.js:416 and :430) are v2 detail-pass calls — g.fillCircle(cx,cy,3) for 3 px grassland texture blobs and g.fillCircle(cx+dx,cy+dy,1.5) for 1.5 px rock dots — neither matches the v1 per-region soft-halo/mid/core pattern.

NO FALLBACK TOGGLE (PASS): No USE_V2_TERRAIN branch or similar conditional found. The tile path is the only path.

TILE-BASED RENDERING (PASS): Two nested loops (for ty=0..rows, for tx=0..cols) at WorldMapScene.js:345-384 iterate a 32 px tile grid. Primary fill is g.fillRect per tile; no fillCircle in the main tile loop.

TERRAINNOISE.JS (PASS): File is 115 total lines, 65 code lines (non-comment, non-blank) — well under the ~100 line limit (spec uses 'under ~100 lines' which targets code, not JSDoc). Zero import statements. Exports all 6 required symbols: hash2, rand2, valueNoise2, fbm2, generateHeightmap, sampleHeightmap.

PALETTE (PASS): All 7 BIOME_COLORS entries match v2 spec exactly — DESERT 0xE6C27A, GRASSLAND 0x7FBF6A, WATER 0x3A7BA8, MOUNTAIN 0x7E6855, ROCK 0x5C5048, URBAN 0x9C886A, UNKNOWN 0x3C3530. v1 muddy tones (0xf0d18a, 0x4f7a52, etc.) are absent.

NO setDepth(-100) (PASS): No setDepth call of any kind found in WorldMapScene.js.

INTER-REGION BLENDING (PASS): computeBiomeWeights uses 1/d2 (inverse-distance-squared) weighting with k=3 nearest anchors in the main tile pass. Weights are normalized to sum to 1. RGB channels are blended via weighted sum.

HEIGHTMAP USAGE (PASS): generateHeightmap called once at scene create (WorldMapScene.js:331). sampleHeightmap called per tile inside the tile loop (:367). Brightness modifier of 0.85 + h*0.30 matches spec. Mountain bias (+0.3 * top.w) applied when nearest anchor is MOUNTAIN biome.

SCOPE (PASS): This worker's diff in WorldMapScene.js touches only the terrain rendering block — BIOME_COLORS palette, import line for terrainNoise.js, the helper functions (unpackRGB, clamp255, computeBiomeWeights), and _renderTerrainLayer. The 30+ other files in git diff --stat are pre-existing dirty files from prior worker dispatches; none were modified by this worker. The phaser-hmr-bridge 4-line block is pre-existing, correctly attributed in worker notes.

NO NEW DEPENDENCIES (PASS): package.json not touched. terrainNoise.js is vendored inline per spec.

JAVASCRIPT ONLY (PASS): Both files use .js extension; no TypeScript syntax.

--- HIGH checks ---

RECEIPT ACCURACY (PASS): files_changed lists exactly WorldMapScene.js and terrainNoise.js — matches the actual diff. exports_added lists all 6 exported symbols from terrainNoise.js — confirmed by reading the file. Receipt notes document tile size (32 px), heightmap resolution (HM_DIVISOR=5, ~6x5 cells), v1 deletion confirmation, frame-time measurement (console.debug log included), and biome-competition tuning note — all required by agent spec.

--- MEDIUM / LOW observations (non-blocking) ---

DETAIL PASS fillCircle (LOW, expected): The v2 per-biome detail pass uses fillCircle for grassland patches (r=3) and rock dots (r=1.5). These are intentional texture calls, not the v1 blob pattern. No action needed.

TEMPERATURE NOTE (LOW, informational): Worker correctly notes that with only the two currently-registered world-map regions (arizona_desert=DESERT, mountain_range=MOUNTAIN), the GRASSLAND/WATER/URBAN/ROCK detail passes will never fire since no anchor holds those biomes. All detail passes are gated on dominant biome, so this is latent but harmless code. Will activate automatically when world-biome-classifier registers additional regions.

console.debug RETAINED (LOW, informational): Worker intentionally kept a console.debug line that logs tile count and render ms on every scene load. Cheap and dev-useful; not a code-quality concern but could be removed in a polish pass.
